title: Pillowy Uruguayan Sevaiiya Empanadas
description: A solo dinner dish from Uruguay, featuring the umami-rich sevaiiya, with an anti-inflammatory twist.
instructions: In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute.
Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour and eggs.
Add the flour mixture to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the salt and black pepper to taste.
Using a small cookie scoop or a spoon, portion out the filling onto a clean surface.
Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the dough to rest.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of the filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-circle and press the edges to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and sprinkle with parsley.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
